protocols.title: Quickdraw timing boundary matrix
protocols.objective: Measure whether issue and pull-request closure intervals produce Quickdraw under legitimate public-repository use.
ethics: Use substantive issues or pull requests only.
Do not flood repositories, create spam, or repeat tests after a maintainer asks you to stop.
prerequisites: A public test repository with normal issue and pull-request functionality.
variables: Object type: issue or pull request.
Elapsed time between creation and closure.
Who creates and who closes the object.
steps: Record the account's visible achievement state before the test.
Create one legitimate issue or pull request and record the UTC creation timestamp.
Close it at the planned interval and record the UTC closure timestamp.
Check profile visibility at bounded intervals without repeatedly changing repository state.
Record the first visible award time or the observation cutoff.
stop_conditions: Stop after the planned observation window.
Stop immediately if repository maintainers object.
privacy_rules: Remove unnecessary usernames from public evidence.
Never submit tokens, private repository links, or private communications.

protocols.title: YOLO review-state matrix
protocols.objective: Separate the effects of no review, comment-only review, requested changes, and approval on YOLO qualification.
ethics: Use substantive pull requests in repositories where self-merge is permitted.
Do not weaken production branch protection merely to obtain an achievement.
prerequisites: A public test repository with documented merge and review settings.
steps: Record current visible achievement state and repository rules.
Create a substantive pull request for one planned review state.
Apply only the planned review state and record its exact type.
Merge using the planned method where repository policy permits.
Record first-visible award time or observation cutoff.
stop_conditions: Do not bypass required reviews or administrator safeguards.
Stop when the planned matrix cell is complete.
privacy_rules: Remove email addresses and authentication material.

protocols.title: Pair Extraordinaire attribution and tier matrix
protocols.objective: Test whether final merged history preserves qualifying co-author attribution and observe tier boundaries without artificial activity.
ethics: Every co-author must have made a real contribution.
Do not add false Co-authored-by trailers or meaningless commits.
prerequisites: A public repository with legitimate collaborative work.
steps: Record the pre-test visible achievement and qualifying-count estimate.
Create a substantive co-authored commit with a valid trailer.
Merge using the planned method.
Inspect the final commit history for both authors.
Record award visibility and any relevant tier boundary.
stop_conditions: Stop if attribution would be false or misleading.
Do not create extra pull requests solely to move a counter.
privacy_rules: Use public noreply emails where possible.
Do not publish private contributor email addresses.

protocols.title: Galaxy Brain accepted-answer trigger
protocols.objective: Verify accepted-answer progress and distinguish it from other Discussion moderation states.
ethics: Use genuine questions and useful answers.
The repository maintainer must decide acceptance independently.
prerequisites: A public repository with GitHub Discussions and answer marking enabled.
variables: Time between acceptance and profile visibility.
steps: Record accepted-answer count and visible achievement state.
Post a genuine answer to a suitable Discussion.
Have the maintainer mark the answer using the planned state.
Record acceptance and first-visible-award timestamps.
Repeat only through normal community participation.
stop_conditions: Do not ask maintainers to mark incorrect answers as accepted.
Stop if the Discussion is removed or participation is unwelcome.
privacy_rules: Do not publish private moderation messages.

protocols.title: Public Sponsor visibility trigger
protocols.objective: Test public sponsorship visibility conditions without collecting or publishing financial data.
ethics: Sponsorship must be genuine and voluntarily chosen.
Never reimburse, coordinate, or fabricate sponsorship activity for testing.
prerequisites: A consenting sponsor account eligible for GitHub Sponsors.
A genuine sponsorship the participant independently wishes to make.
steps: Record the pre-sponsorship visible achievement state.
Create or identify a genuine sponsorship and record only its public visibility state.
Record activation time without recording payment details.
Observe profile award visibility at bounded intervals.
If visibility changes, record the change and relevant non-financial settings.
stop_conditions: Do not collect payment method, amount, billing address, or transaction identifiers.
Stop if the participant withdraws consent.
privacy_rules: Publish only the minimum public sponsorship state needed for review.
